sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「窬，小竇也，音踰。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Yu means a small hole, read yu."
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu explains the word for a thief-tunnel as a small hole, with the reading given in fanqie spelling.

sentences.chinese: 繇讀與由同。
sentences.literal: You is read the same as you.
sentences.idiomatic: The graph is read like the homophone meaning "from" or "thereby."

sentences.chinese: 下皆類此。」
sentences.literal: What follows below is all of this kind."
sentences.idiomatic: The same glossing convention applies below."

sentences.chinese: 〉臣謹桉軍法曰：『正亡屬將軍，將軍有罪以聞，〈師古曰：「言軍正不屬將軍。
sentences.literal: 〉Your servant respectfully according to military law says: 'The chief rectifier does not belong to the general; if the general has a crime, one may report it to be heard. 〈Yan's note: "It means the army rectifier does not belong to the general."
sentences.idiomatic: Your servant cites the code: the chief judge answers to the throne, not the camp commander; a general's crimes go up by memorial. Yan Shigu stresses that the rectifier is not the general's subordinate."

sentences.chinese: 將軍有罪過，得表奏之。」
sentences.literal: If the general has faults, one may memorialize them."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Any fault on the general's part may be tabled for the emperor."

sentences.chinese: 〉二千石以下行法焉。』
sentences.literal: 'Those of two thousand piculs and below carry out the law.'"
sentences.idiomatic: Officials at two thousand piculs and below enforce the statute."

sentences.chinese: 〈孟康曰：「二千石謂軍中校尉、都尉之屬。」
sentences.literal: 〈Meng Kang's note: "Two thousand piculs means colonels and chief commandants in the army."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Meng Kang clarifies: here "two thousand piculs" covers colonels and chief commandants.

sentences.chinese: 〉丞於用法疑，〈孟康曰：「丞屬軍正，斬御史於法有疑。」
sentences.literal: 〉As assistant I had doubt in applying the law. 〈Meng Kang's note: "The assistant belongs to the chief rectifier; beheading the clerk was doubtful in law."〉
sentences.idiomatic: As deputy I hesitated at the law's edge—Meng Kang notes the deputy served the chief rectifier, so killing a censor sat in a gray zone.

sentences.chinese: 〉執事不諉上，〈師古曰：「諉，累也。
sentences.literal: 〉One who executes affairs does not shirk onto superiors. 〈Yan's note: "Wei means 'to burden.'
sentences.idiomatic: The officer on the spot must act when the code demands—not pass the buck upward. Yan Shigu glosses wei as "burden" or "implicate."

sentences.chinese: 言執事者，當見法即行，不可以事累於上也。
sentences.literal: The meaning is that executors, when they see the law, should act immediately; they may not burden superiors with the affair.
sentences.idiomatic: Execute the law at once; do not drag the throne into it."

sentences.chinese: 諉音女瑞反。
sentences.literal: Wei is read nu-rui fan.
sentences.idiomatic: The character wei is spelled fanqie as nu-rui.

sentences.chinese: 累音力瑞反。」
sentences.literal: Lei is read li-rui fan.""
sentences.idiomatic: The character lei is spelled fanqie as li-rui."

sentences.chinese: 〉臣謹以斬，昧死以聞。」
sentences.literal: 〉Your servant respectfully therefore struck him off, risking death to report."
sentences.idiomatic: Your servant therefore took his head and reports at peril of his life."

sentences.chinese: 制曰：「司馬法曰『國容不入軍，軍容不入國』，何文吏也？
sentences.literal: Imperial rescript: "The Sima Fa says 'state deportment does not enter the army; army deportment does not enter the state'—why [cite] civil clerks?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor answered: "The Sima canons say camp law is not capital etiquette—why drag in civil judges?"

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「司馬法亦兵書之名也，解在主父偃傳。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Sima Fa is also the name of a military book; the explanation is in the biography of Zhufu Yan.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: Sima Fa is a military treatise, as explained under Zhufu Yan."

sentences.chinese: 詔言在於軍中，何用文吏議也。」
sentences.literal: The edict's meaning is that it lies within the army—why use civil clerk deliberation?"〉
sentences.idiomatic: The rescript puts the case under field law, not the ministry clerks."

sentences.chinese: 〉三王或誓於軍中，欲民先成其慮也；
sentences.literal: The Three Kings sometimes swore oaths in the army, wishing the people first to complete their planning;
sentences.idiomatic: The three sage kings swore inside the camp so every soldier fixed his plan before battle;

sentences.chinese: 或誓於軍門之外，欲民先意以待事也；
sentences.literal: or swore outside the army gate, wishing the people first to set their intent while awaiting affairs;
sentences.idiomatic: or they swore beyond the gate so the host steeled its will before the order came;

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「慮謂計念也。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Lu means calculation and thought.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: lu is forethought, calculation."

sentences.chinese: 先意謂先爲之意也。」
sentences.literal: Xian yi means first to establish intent."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Xian yi means shaping resolve beforehand."

sentences.chinese: 〉或將交刃而誓，致民志也。』
sentences.literal: or when about to cross blades they swore, fixing the people's will.'"
sentences.idiomatic: or they swore on the eve of steel to lock the army's courage."

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「欲致民勇志，使不奔北。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Wishing to fix the people's brave will so they do not flee north."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: the oath was meant to nerve the ranks so none broke and ran."

sentences.chinese: 〉建又何疑焉？」
sentences.literal: What more doubt has Jian?"
sentences.idiomatic: What doubt can Hu Jian still raise?"

sentences.chinese: 建繇是顯名。
sentences.literal: Jian thereby became famous.
sentences.idiomatic: Hu Jian's name rang through the empire.

sentences.chinese: 後爲渭城令，治甚有聲。
sentences.literal: Later he was magistrate of Weicheng; his governance had great reputation.
sentences.idiomatic: He later governed Weicheng county and earned a ringing reputation.

sentences.chinese: 值昭帝幼，皇后父上官將軍安與帝姊蓋主私夫丁外人相善。
sentences.literal: It happened that Emperor Zhao was young, and the empress's father, General Shangguan An, was intimate with the Princess of Gai's paramour Ding Wairen, the empress's elder sister's lover.
sentences.idiomatic: Young Zhao sat the throne while the empress's father, General Shangguan An, consorted with Ding Wairen, lover of the emperor's sister the Princess of Gai.

sentences.chinese: 外人驕恣，怨故京兆尹樊福，使客射殺之。
sentences.literal: Wairen was arrogant and unrestrained; he resented the former capital governor Fan Fu and sent retainers to shoot and kill him.
sentences.idiomatic: Ding Wairen was insolent, hated the ex-governor Fan Fu, and had him shot by hired blades.

sentences.chinese: 客臧公主廬，吏不敢捕。
sentences.literal: The retainers hid in the princess's lodge, and the clerks dared not arrest them.
sentences.idiomatic: The killers sheltered in the princess's compound; no constable dared touch them.

sentences.chinese: 渭城令建將吏卒圍捕。
sentences.literal: The magistrate of Weicheng, Jian, led clerks and soldiers to surround and capture them.
sentences.idiomatic: Magistrate Hu Jian ringed the house with troops and moved to arrest.

sentences.chinese: 蓋主聞之，與外人、上官將軍多從奴客往，犇射追吏，〈師古曰：「犇，古奔字也。
sentences.literal: When the Princess of Gai heard it, she with Wairen and General Shangguan, with many slave-followers and retainers, went; they galloped and shot, pursuing the clerks. 〈Yan's note: "Ben is the ancient character for run."
sentences.idiomatic: The princess rode out with Ding, Shangguan, and a mob of clients, galloping and shooting at Hu's men—Yan Shigu notes ben is the old graph for "run."

sentences.chinese: 奔走赴之而射也。」
sentences.literal: Running and hastening to them, they shot."〉
sentences.idiomatic: They charged in shooting."

sentences.chinese: 〉吏散走。
sentences.literal: The clerks scattered and fled.
sentences.idiomatic: Hu's detachment broke and ran.

sentences.chinese: 主使僕射劾渭城令游徼傷主家奴。
sentences.literal: The princess ordered her chief steward to impeach the Weicheng patrol officer for wounding her household slaves.
sentences.idiomatic: She had her majordomo indict the county patrol for wounding her slaves.

sentences.chinese: 建報亡它坐。
sentences.literal: Jian reported that there was no other offense.
sentences.idiomatic: Hu Jian answered that the patrol had committed no separate crime.

sentences.chinese: 〈服虔曰：「言游徼奉公，無它坐也。」
sentences.literal: 〈Fu Qian's note: "It means the patrol officer acted on public duty; there was no other offense."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Fu Qian reads the line as praise of the patrol's lawful conduct.

sentences.chinese: 〉蓋主怒，使人上書告建侵辱長公主，射甲舍門。
sentences.literal: The Princess of Gai was angry and had someone memorialize accusing Jian of insulting a senior princess and shooting the gate of the armor hall.
sentences.idiomatic: She struck back with a memorial accusing Hu Jian of lese-majeste and riddling the gate of her first-ranked mansion.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「甲舍即甲第，公主之宅。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Jia she is jia di, the princess's mansion."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: "armor hall" is another name for a nobleman's top-tier residence.

sentences.chinese: 〉知吏賊傷奴，辟報故不窮審。
sentences.literal: Knowing that clerks had wrongfully injured slaves, he gave a false return and therefore did not exhaustively investigate. 〈Su Lin's note: "Pi means to turn aside.
sentences.idiomatic: Hu knew his men had bloodied her slaves yet filed a dodgy closing report and closed the case. Su Lin thought pi meant "turn aside."

sentences.chinese: 〈蘇林曰：「辟，迴也。
sentences.literal: Bao means verdict.
sentences.idiomatic: Bao means "verdict" or "finding."

sentences.chinese: 報，論也。
sentences.literal: Deciding a prison case is bao.
sentences.idiomatic: Closing a criminal file is called bao.

sentences.chinese: 斷獄爲報。
sentences.literal: Therefore the speech means there was a pretext.
sentences.idiomatic: Su read it as "there was a stated reason."

sentences.chinese: 故言有故也。
sentences.literal: Not exhaustively investigating means not exhausting the affair."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Not to investigate exhaustively is to leave the matter half done."

sentences.chinese: 不窮審，不窮盡其事也。」
sentences.literal: Yan's note: "Su's explanation is wrong.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu rejects Su Lin.

sentences.chinese: 師古曰：「蘇說非也。
sentences.literal: It means that for the patrol officer he avoided guilt and falsely returned documents, and therefore was not exhaustively prosecuted."
sentences.idiomatic: Hu means the patrol dodged blame with a sham report, so the case never went to trial."

sentences.chinese: 言爲游徼避罪而妄報文書，故不窮治也。
sentences.literal: Pi is read bi."〉
sentences.idiomatic: The character pi is read like bi, "avoid."

sentences.chinese: 辟讀曰避。」
sentences.literal: Grand General Huo Guang shelved his memorial.
sentences.idiomatic: Huo Guang buried the princess's bill.

sentences.chinese: 〉大將軍霍光寢其奏。
sentences.literal: Later when Guang fell ill, the Shangguan clan handled affairs in his stead; they sent subordinates to arrest Jian, and Jian committed suicide.
sentences.idiomatic: When Huo fell sick the Shangguans seized the reins, ordered Hu Jian arrested, and he killed himself.

sentences.chinese: 後光病，上官氏代聽事，下吏捕建，建自殺。
sentences.literal: Clerks and commoners called it injustice; to this day Weicheng has established his shrine.
sentences.idiomatic: The people of Weicheng still call it murder and keep a shrine to his memory.

sentences.chinese: 吏民稱冤，至今渭城立其祠。
sentences.literal: Zhu Yun, courtesy name You, was a man of Lu who moved to Pingling.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun, courtesy You, was a Lu man who resettled at Pingling.

sentences.chinese: 朱雲字游，魯人也，徙平陵。
sentences.literal: In youth he consorted with knights-errant and light fellows, borrowing retainers to avenge grudges.
sentences.idiomatic: As a youth he ran with swordsmen and hired blades for revenge.

sentences.chinese: 少時通輕俠，借客報仇。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Jie means to assist, read zi-ye fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu glosses jie as "lend aid," with fanqie spelling zi-ye.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「借，助也，音子夜反。」
sentences.literal: He was more than eight feet tall, of very martial appearance, and was famed for courage and strength.
sentences.idiomatic: He stood over eight feet, looked every inch a fighter, and was known for raw strength.

sentences.chinese: 〉長八尺餘，容貌甚壯，以勇力聞。
sentences.literal: At age forty he then changed his course and studied the Changes with Erudite Bai Ziyou, and also studied the Analects with former General Xiao Wangzhi—both could transmit their learning.
sentences.idiomatic: At forty he reformed, studied the Zhou yi under Doctor Bai Ziyou and the Analects under the retired general Xiao Wangzhi, and mastered both teachers.

sentences.chinese: 年四十，迺變節從博士白子友受易，又事前將軍蕭望之受論語，皆能傳其業。
sentences.literal: He loved grand integrity and unconstrained spirit. 〈Yan's note: "Ti is read tu-li fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: He admired heroic independence—Yan Shigu gives the fanqie for ti.

sentences.chinese: 好倜儻大節，〈師古曰：「倜音吐歷反。」
sentences.literal: The age on this account esteemed him.
sentences.idiomatic: The capital set great store by him.

sentences.chinese: 〉當世以是高之。
sentences.literal: Under Emperor Yuan, Gong Yu of Langya was Imperial Counselor, while the acting guardian of Huayin, chief clerk Jia, submitted a sealed memorial. 〈Yan's note: "He is the man named Jia who was acting chief clerk of Huayin county."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Under Yuan, Imperial Counselor Gong Yu of Langya received a sealed memorial from Jia, acting county chief clerk of Huayin—Yan Shigu names the man Jia.

sentences.chinese: 元帝時，琅邪貢禹爲御史大夫，而華陰守丞嘉上封事，〈師古曰：「守華陰縣丞者，其人名嘉。」
sentences.literal: 〉saying 'the way of good order lies in obtaining the worthy; the office of Imperial Counselor is the chancellor's deputy, to the right of the nine ministers—〈Yan's note: "You means above."〉
sentences.idiomatic: It argued that order rests on worthy ministers, that the censor is the chancellor's right hand and outranks the nine ministers—Yan Shigu reads you as "senior."

sentences.chinese: 〉言『治道在於得賢，御史之官，宰相之副，九卿之右，〈師古曰：「右言在上也。」
sentences.literal: it cannot but be carefully chosen.
sentences.idiomatic: and must be filled with care.

sentences.chinese: 〉不可不選。
sentences.literal: Zhu Yun of Pingling combines civil and military talent, is loyal, upright, and has strategy; he may be tried as acting Imperial Counselor at six hundred piculs salary to exhaust his ability.'"
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun of Pingling blends civil and martial gifts with loyal wit; try him as acting imperial counselor at six hundred piculs and see what he can do."

sentences.chinese: 平陵朱雲，兼資文武，忠正有智略，可使以六百石秩試守御史大夫，以盡其能。」
sentences.literal: Thereupon the emperor sent the matter down to ask the dukes and ministers.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor bounced the memorial to the high assembly.

sentences.chinese: 上迺下其事問公卿。
sentences.literal: Junior Tutor to the Heir Kuang Heng replied, holding that "great ministers are the state's thighs and arms, what the myriad surnames look up to, whom an enlightened king chooses with care.
sentences.idiomatic: Junior tutor Kuang Heng answered that great ministers are the state's limbs, the people's mirror, and a wise king picks them with utmost care.

sentences.chinese: 太子少傅匡衡對，以爲「大臣者，國家之股肱，萬姓所瞻仰，明王所慎擇也。
sentences.literal: The tradition says: if those below lightly esteem their superiors' ranks and base men plot against ministers who hold the handle, then the state trembles and the people are not quiet.
sentences.idiomatic: The classics warn that when commoners despise high rank and plot against power-holders, the realm shakes.

sentences.chinese: 傳曰下輕其上爵，賤人圖柄臣，則國家搖動而民不靜矣。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Shang jue means great offices.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: shang jue means exalted posts."

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「上爵，大官也。
sentences.literal: Tu means to plot.
sentences.idiomatic: Tu is "to scheme."

sentences.chinese: 圖，謀也。
sentences.literal: Bing chen means a minister who holds power."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Bing chen is a minister who wields the levers of power."

sentences.chinese: 柄臣，執權之臣。」
sentences.literal: Now Jia, starting from acting chief clerk, plots the place of a great minister, wishing with the body of a commoner on foot to leap above the nine ministers—it is not the way to give weight to the state and honor the altars of soil and grain.
sentences.idiomatic: Yet Jia, a mere acting clerk, would vault a foot soldier into the censor's chair—not how you steady the altars.

sentences.chinese: 〉今嘉從守丞而圖大臣之位，欲以匹夫徒步之人而超九卿之右，非所以重國家而尊社稷也。
sentences.literal: From Yao's employment of Shun down to King Wen's use of the Grand Duke, they still examined carefully and then enfeoffed them—how much the more in the case of Zhu Yun?
sentences.idiomatic: Even Yao with Shun and King Wen with the Grand Duke tested them long before handing seals—would Zhu Yun be rushed faster?

sentences.chinese: 自堯之用舜，文王於太公，猶試然後爵之，又況朱雲者乎？
sentences.literal: Yun has always loved courage, repeatedly violated the law and fled for his life; in receiving the Changes he has some teacherly method, but in conduct and righteousness there is nothing to distinguish him.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu loves a fight, has fled the law more than once, knows the Changes well enough to teach, yet shows nothing singular in conduct.

sentences.chinese: 雲素好勇，數犯法亡命，受易頗有師道，其行義未有以異。
sentences.literal: Now Imperial Counselor Yu is pure, white, incorrupt, and upright, his classical learning clear and penetrating, with the air of Boyi and Ziyu of Wei—none within the seas fails to hear and know—yet Jia vulgarly praises Yun, 〈Yan's note: "Wei means 'crooked.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: Imperial Counselor Gong Yu is stainless, learned, and as straight as Boyi or Ziyu—everyone knows it—yet Jia crookedly praises Zhu Yun. Yan Shigu: wei means "twisted" or "improper."

sentences.chinese: 今御史大夫禹絜白廉正，經術通明，有伯夷、史魚之風，海內莫不聞知，而嘉猥稱雲，〈師古曰：「猥，曲也。」
sentences.literal: wishing to make him Imperial Counselor, recklessly recommending each other—I suspect a treacherous heart; such gradual encroachment must not be allowed to grow; it is fitting to send it down to the authorities to investigate and verify, to clarify good and evil.'"
sentences.idiomatic: He would make Zhu censor by mutual puffery—smelling conspiracy, Kuang asked for a full inquest before the rot spreads."

sentences.chinese: 〉欲令爲御史大夫，妄相稱舉，疑有姦心，漸不可長，宜下有司案驗以明好惡。」
sentences.literal: In the end Jia sat guilty for it.
sentences.idiomatic: Jia paid for the stunt with his career.

sentences.chinese: 嘉竟坐之。
sentences.literal: At that time Junior Minister Wulu Chongzong was noble and favored, and lectured on the Liangqiu school's Changes.
sentences.idiomatic: Meanwhile Junior Minister Wulu Chongzong stood high at court expounding the Liangqiu interpretation of the Changes.

sentences.chinese: 是時，少府五鹿充宗貴幸，爲梁丘易。
sentences.literal: From Emperor Xuandi's time they had been good at the Liangqiu explanations; Emperor Yuan loved them and wished to examine their sameness and difference, ordering Chongzong to debate with the various Changes scholars.
sentences.idiomatic: Since Xuandi the court had favored Liangqiu; Yuan ordered a showdown between Chongzong and every other school.

sentences.chinese: 自宣帝時善梁丘氏說，元帝好之，欲考其異同，令充宗與諸易家論。
sentences.literal: Chongzong rode on his nobility and a sharp tongue. 〈Yan's note: "Cheng means 'because of.'
sentences.idiomatic: Chongzong traded on rank and a glib tongue—Yan Shigu glosses cheng as "ride on" or "trade on."

sentences.chinese: 充宗乘貴辯口，〈師古曰：「乘，因也。
sentences.literal: It means relying on the power of nobility and honor."〉
sentences.idiomatic: He leaned on imperial favor to cow the doctors."

sentences.chinese: 言因藉尊貴之權也。」
sentences.literal: The various scholars none could withstand him; all claimed illness and dared not meet.
sentences.idiomatic: Every scholar feigned sickness rather than face him.

sentences.chinese: 〉諸儒莫能與抗，皆稱疾不敢會。
sentences.literal: Someone recommended Yun; he was summoned in, tucked his lower robe and ascended the hall. 〈Yan's note: "Zi is the lower skirt of the garment, read zi-si fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Someone nominated Zhu Yun; he strode in, tucked his court skirt, and mounted the dais—Yan Shigu identifies zi as the lower robe, fanqie zi-si.

sentences.chinese: 有薦雲者，召入，攝𪗋登堂，〈師古曰：「𪗋，衣下之裳，音子私反。」
sentences.literal: 〉He raised his head and requested. 〈Yan's note: "Kang means 'to raise.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: He lifted his head to speak—Yan Shigu: kang is "raise."

sentences.chinese: 〉抗首而請，〈師古曰：「抗，舉也。」
sentences.literal: His voice moved those left and right.
sentences.idiomatic: His voice shook the hall.

sentences.chinese: 〉音動左右。
sentences.literal: When the debate and cross-examination were finished, he repeatedly rebuffed Lord Wulu. 〈Yan's note: "Zhu means to stab, to block, read zhu-yu fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: In disputation he pinned Chongzong again and again—Yan Shigu glosses zhu as "gore" or "parry," fanqie zhu-yu.

sentences.chinese: 旣論難，連拄五鹿君，〈師古曰：「拄，刺也，距也，音竹庾反。」
sentences.literal: Therefore the various scholars made a saying of it: 'Wulu stands horned tall; Zhu Yun breaks his horn.'"
sentences.idiomatic: The doctors sang: "Wulu locks horns high—Zhu Yun snaps his tip."

sentences.chinese: 〉故諸儒爲之語曰：「五鹿嶽嶽，朱雲折其角。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Yueyue is the appearance of long horns."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: yueyue paints tall branching horns.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「嶽嶽，長角之貌。」
sentences.literal: Thereupon he became an erudite.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor named him an erudite.

sentences.chinese: 〉繇是爲博士。
sentences.literal: He was transferred to be magistrate of Duling; for intentionally releasing fugitives he sat guilty, but it met an amnesty; he was recommended as upright and square and became magistrate of Huaili.
sentences.idiomatic: He moved to Duling magistrate, fell for letting fugitives slip, then was pardoned, recommended as upright, and sent to Huaili.

sentences.chinese: 遷杜陵令，坐故縱亡命，會赦，舉方正，爲槐里令。
sentences.literal: At that time Palace Secretary Shi Xian held power; he formed a faction with Chongzong, and the hundred officials feared them.
sentences.idiomatic: Palace secretary Shi Xian ran the inner court in league with Chongzong, and the bureaucracy trembled.

sentences.chinese: 時中書令石顯用事，與充宗爲黨，百僚畏之。
sentences.literal: Only Palace Secretary to the Imperial Counselor Chen Xian, though young, upheld integrity, did not attach to Xian and the rest, but formed ties with Yun.
sentences.idiomatic: Only young Chen Xian, assistant censor, defied Shi Xian and befriended Zhu Yun.

sentences.chinese: 唯御史中丞陳咸年少抗節，不附顯等，而與雲相結。
sentences.literal: Only Palace Secretary to the Imperial Counselor Chen Xian, though young, upheld integrity, did not attach to Xian and the rest, but formed ties with Yun.
sentences.idiomatic: Only young Chen Xian, assistant censor, defied Shi Xian and befriended Zhu Yun.

sentences.chinese: 雲數上疏，言丞相韋玄成容身保位，亡能往來，〈李竒曰：「不能有所前卻也。」
sentences.literal: Yun repeatedly submitted memorials saying Chancellor Wei Xuancheng preserved his person and held his place, unable to advance or retreat. 〈Li Qi's note: "Unable to have any forward or backward movement."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun memorialized that Chancellor Wei Xuancheng clung to his cushion, neither advancing policy nor yielding—Li Qi reads the phrase as "unable to move either way."

sentences.chinese: 師古曰：「周書君奭之篇稱周公曰：『惟文王尚克修和有夏，有若虢叔、閎夭、散宜生、泰顛、南宮括。』
sentences.literal: Yan's note: "In the 'Jun Shi' chapter of the Zhou Documents the Duke of Zhou says: 'Only King Wen was able to cultivate harmony with the Xia; there were such as Guo Shu, Hong Yao, San Yisheng, Tai Dian, Nangong Kuo.'
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu quotes the "Jun Shi" chapter: the Duke of Zhou praises King Wen's ministers—Guo Shu, Hong Yao, San Yisheng, Tai Dian, and Nangong Kuo.

sentences.chinese: 又曰『亡能往來』。
sentences.literal: It also says 'unable to advance or retreat.'"
sentences.idiomatic: The same passage speaks of men who could neither press forward nor step back."

sentences.chinese: 故雲引此以爲言也。」
sentences.literal: Therefore Yun cited this to speak."〉
sentences.idiomatic: That is the line Zhu Yun invoked against Wei Xuancheng."

sentences.chinese: 〉而咸數毀石顯。
sentences.literal: Moreover Xian repeatedly slandered Shi Xian.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Xian meanwhile kept impeaching Shi Xian before the throne.

sentences.chinese: 乆之，有司考雲，疑風吏殺人。
sentences.literal: After a long while the authorities examined Yun, suspecting he had hinted clerks to kill people.
sentences.idiomatic: Months later the authorities reopened Zhu Yun's file, suspecting he had urged underlings to kill.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「風讀曰諷。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Feng is read feng 'to hint.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu reads the character as feng, meaning to hint or suggest indirectly.

sentences.chinese: 〉羣臣朝見，上問丞相以雲治行。
sentences.literal: When the ministers met in court audience, the emperor asked the chancellor about Yun's conduct in governing.
sentences.idiomatic: At the next great audience Emperor Yuan asked the chancellor how Zhu Yun had governed.

sentences.chinese: 丞相玄成言雲暴虐亡狀。
sentences.literal: Chancellor Xuancheng said Yun was cruel and violent without good aspect.
sentences.idiomatic: Wei Xuancheng replied that Zhu Yun was savage and had left no creditable record.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「無善狀也。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "There was no good appearance."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu glosses "no good aspect" as no praiseworthy conduct to report.

sentences.chinese: 〉時陳咸在前，聞之，以語雲。
sentences.literal: 〉At that time Chen Xian was in front; hearing it, he told Yun.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Xian stood in the front ranks, overheard the answer, and warned Zhu Yun.

sentences.chinese: 雲上書自訟，咸爲定奏草，求下御史中丞。
sentences.literal: Yun submitted a memorial defending himself; Xian drafted the memorial for him, seeking to send it down to the Palace Secretary to the Imperial Counselor.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun filed a memorial in his own defense; Chen Xian composed the draft and asked that it be referred to the assistant censor.

sentences.chinese: 事下丞相，丞相部吏考立其殺人罪。
sentences.literal: The affair was sent down to the chancellor; the chancellor's subordinate clerks examined and established his crime of murder.
sentences.idiomatic: The chancellor's yamen ran the inquest until clerks could enter a murder conviction in the record.
